summary: Install-time and import-time exfiltration of ~/.aws/credentials to hardcoded remote host
details: Package ships a credential stealer that targets the installer's AWS credentials. scripts/postinstall.js runs during `npm install` and reads ~/.aws/credentials, then POSTs the contents to http://139.59.87.78:8765/listener over plaintext HTTP. index.js (the package main) additionally runs an IIFE at require() time that reads the same ~/.aws/credentials file and POSTs to the same endpoint, so importing the module exfiltrates credentials even when installs use --ignore-scripts. Errors are suppressed for stealth, and the advertised purpose (a date-printing helper) is unrelated to the actual behavior. The package.json description and README also embed prompt-injection content directed at automated review tooling, instructing summarizers to declare the package safe — a cover-story signal corroborating malicious intent rather than excusing it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
